programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: The Mutual UFO Network is an organization devoted to the scientific investigation of unidentified aerial phenomena and other unexplained encounters; with more than five decades of investigations, MUFON's extensive database could contain proof.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Strange rock carvings from 8,000 B.C.; unexplained sightings in the new world; a frightening anomaly plaguing the American west; a strange man-made cave filled with ancient objects and mummies; strange lights and unexplained weather anomalies.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: The native Hawaiians trace their ancestry to beings from the Pleiades star system called the Akua; a look at these islands and if they once had been inhabited by extraterrestrials.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Angkor Wat, Cambodia, is the world's largest ancient religious temple.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: For thousands of years, people have held the belief that everyday objects can possess hidden energy; talismans are seen to provide protection, good fortune, and some are thought to embody a higher power.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Probes have been sending information about Mars back to scientists for nearly 40 years. Photographs of the planet reveal mysteries that could mean life on Mars.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Shocking new evidence reveals that advanced civilizations might have existed on Earth tens of thousands of years ago; is it possible that a technologically advanced society thrived on Earth, and did they leave behind a record.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Discussing whether ancient cultures could have encountered extraterrestrial beings that have achieved eternal life, and discussing the Ancient Astronaut Theorists suggestions.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Stories of strange encounters during major events in history and myths containing gods that intervene in human affairs may be early accounts of alien encounters.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Extraordinary feats of construction, created without the use of advanced tools or technology, can be found from the jungles of Costa Rica to England's rural countryside.

programme.desc: Exploring the theory that mankind's technological savvy comes from the work of extraterrestrials that watch Earth from outer space; inside the Inca legend, The Book of Enoch, that describes the Watchers descending to Earth to aid our advancement.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: In almost every culture, there are stories of opposing forces at work here on Earth; forces of a supernatural or otherworldly nature.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: A look at the possibility that the human brain possesses extraordinary untapped abilities granted by extraterrestrial beings -- such as telepathy, precognition and psychokinesis; abilities that will enable the next step in the evolution of humans.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Lurking in the oceans are creatures that defy explanation, such as octopuses that can edit their own DNA or animals that can regenerate their entire brain; examining if the oceans are acting as a giant petri dish for alien genetic experimentation.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: In numerous sacred traditions, the number 12 holds a divine significance; it can be found repeated in ancient architecture, groups of 12 wisdom keepers and even the latest scientific principles.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: According to the earliest Tibetan traditions, Shambhala, the kingdom of the gods, is hidden high in the Himalaya Mountains; examining if it's possible that this mystical realm truly exists.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: The mysterious symbol of the keyhole exists in mystic texts, sacred architecture and ancient artwork found all over the planet; exploring if this cryptic shape represents a profound connection to the cosmos.

programme.title: Nightwatch

programme.title: Cold Case Files

programme.title: Cold Case Files
programme.desc: When Sherry Leighty goes missing in 1999, her family is left wondering how a dedicated mother of three could vanish without a trace. A decade later, the cold case heats up.

programme.title: Cold Case Files Classic

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: An intruder breaks into a lawyer's home, murders his wife and blinds him; in a search for the truth, the lawyer questions his troubled son as a possible suspect.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: The father of an Alabama teenager calls the police after he returns home to find his son had thrown a party, leaving the place trashed; 11 minutes after the deputy leaves the house, another 911 call is placed reporting a shooting at the residence.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: A scuba diving trip ends in tragedy; officials identify a man's remains more than 20 years after his mysterious disappearance.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: A look at the case of con man John Meehan, including interviews with his wife and her daughter, his ex-wife, law enforcement officers, and the Los Angeles Times reporter who shined a spotlight on the story.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: Against all odds, a young woman is found alive after an attack on her and a friend near Corpus Christi, Texas.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: When a California housewife is murdered in broad daylight, the suspected killer claims it was a burglary gone awry.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: When a determined investigator works with a prosecutor in hope of solving a 20-year-old murder case, a dark secret is uncovered.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: A woman comes forward with the truth about the deaths of three other women, solving a 17-year-old mystery; interviews with two people at the center of the case.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: A Kentucky man is convicted of murdering his co-worker; the mystery is considered solved until a stunning disclosure years later turns the closed case upside down.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: When a mother of five is found dead in her bedroom, her family believes a crazed killer is to blame.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: After losing a hotly contested custody battle, a woman disappears with her 11-month-old daughter; the child's father searches four continents for almost two decades before reaching an emotional conclusion.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: An advocate for missing and murdered Indigenous women searches for answers in the disappearance of her niece and exposes cracks in law enforcement.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: The survivor of a horrible attack is convinced police have the wrong suspect.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: The children of an Oklahoma pastor survive the robbery that took their parents' lives and observe the trials of those accused of committing the crime.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: Authorities become suspicious when a pastor loses a second wife in an accident.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: An investigation of abuse allegations against one of the owners of religious reform school Circle of Hope.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: Authorities investigate the killing of a small-town fire chief.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: When a wife calls 911 to report her husband has been shot on a country road in Texas, officers rush to the scene.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: As detectives struggle to find leads in the murder of a college student, the perpetrator strikes again.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: A mother is found dead in a house fire but an autopsy reveals a brutal murder; the case goes cold for years until a shocking confession is made.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: When a woman is abducted during her evening shift at a gift shop, her family keeps the case in the public eye for decades, until investigators finally solve the mystery.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: When a dog trainer with celebrity clientele disappears, investigators track down a suspect with the help of two witnesses.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: A 21-year-old college student is found murdered; the case remains cold for nearly 40 years until a new detective uses an innovative method to catch her killer.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: When remains are found amid the rubble after a house fire, authorities soon discover the incident was no accident.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: A mother searches for answers in the unsolved murder of her 14-year-old daughter until new DNA technology changes everything.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: Lester Holt reports on the mysterious 2005 disappearance of Ray Gricar, a district attorney in Pennsylvania who investigated sex-abuse claims made against Jerry Sandusky in 1998.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: A Florida father is determined to find out what happened to his daughter after her fatal fall from a penthouse apartment in Belgium.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: When a graduate student is shot dead outside of her North Carolina home, a 911 caller leads detectives to believe the victim's fiance is the key to solving the case.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: A mother of three married to a missionary is found dead on Valentine's Day; the investigation into the murder reveals answers close to home.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: Compelling mysteries, powerful documentaries, in-depth investigations.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: A woman goes for a walk during a snowstorm and never returns.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: The investigation into a newlywed's disappearance focuses on her job as a CIA employee as a possible clue.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: The discovery of an Army captain's death in her El Paso, Texas, home shakes her tight-knit military community.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: Years after a woman is murdered in the back of her comic book store, new evidence is discovered that sends the investigation in a surprising direction.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: When Leslie Neulander dies after what appears to be an accidental fall in the shower in her New York home, two friends join forces to seek justice on her behalf; insiders speak out on the latest twists and the final chapter in the case.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: A San Diego mother suddenly vanishes in 2014; nearly two years later, her body is found in the San Diego Bay, but medical examiners say she has only been dead for a month or two.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: After police discover the burned body of a missing Miami man at the edge of the Florida Everglades, the wife of a local supermarket mogul comes forward to reveal a shocking secret and possible lead.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: As actress Aasha Davis' career takes off, her sister and brother-in-law go missing.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: A former ballerina shoots her husband in what she alleges was an act of self-defense; the ensuing investigation and dramatic trial spark protests and receives national attention.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: When a man claims to have found his wife dead in the bathtub, the woman's daughters suspect there may be more to the story.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: A family begins a relentless pursuit of justice after the death of Nevada attorney Susan Winters is ruled a suicide; Susan's parents open up about the seven-year case and their efforts to push back against the criminal justice system.

programme.title: For My Man
programme.desc: When a young mother starts a family with the man of her dreams, she believes nothing will tear them apart. But when her man puts a child�s life at risk, she has to choose between protecting the one she loves and listening to her maternal instincts.

programme.title: Fatal Attraction-Dupe
programme.desc: When a young woman disappears, her friends begin to worry. But then a complicated marriage dynamic is revealed, and police must question if she left voluntarily or if it was something more sinister. The answer will redefine the meaning of evil.

programme.title: Fatal Attraction-Dupe
programme.desc: Police learn that a pregnant 16-year-old vanished from school and suspect that she ran away with her baby�s father. Detectives race to find her, they uncover a killer hiding in plain sight and a horrific secret that will change her family forever.

programme.title: ATL Homicide
programme.desc: Quinn and Vince get a lead on Nacole's case and learn about another cold case from 2005. Solving these cases proves to be a test of patience, but as retirement draws near, the detectives begin to wonder: will Nacole's case ever be solved?

programme.title: Atlanta Homicide
programme.desc: Quinn and Velazquez uncover the secret life of a young mother when she is found murdered, the victim of a psychopathic killer preying on high class prostiitutes.

programme.title: Payback
programme.desc: Two months after a man is acquitted of a murder, he too becomes a victim of gun violence. While police are convinced the shooting was revenge, they and the rest of the community are shocked to learn his own cousin helped lure him to his death.

programme.title: Sins of the City
programme.desc: In the early morning of New Year�s Day 2009, Oakland resident Oscar Grant is shot in the back by a BART transit officer at Fruitvale Station. Within days, dramatic cell phone videos of Oscar�s shooting go public, igniting a global fight for justice.

programme.title: Fatal Attraction-Dupe
programme.desc: After a hail of gunfire leaves two people dead, police quickly identify a suspect, but they struggle to pin him for the crime until a strange coincidence makes them realize he’s already in their crosshairs for another murder.

programme.title: Fatal Attraction-Dupe
programme.desc: When a young woman is found dead in a fire just hours after her grandmother’s death, police suspect her grief may have led to suicide. Learning the victim was pregnant shifts focus to her boyfriend, and reveals a love triangle turned deadly.

programme.title: Fatal Attraction-Dupe
programme.desc: When an aspiring musician and soon-to-be father is stabbed in the street, his girlfriend suspects it was a neighborhood rival. However, Detectives dig into the events of that night and they uncover evidence that leads them straight to their killer.

programme.title: For My Man
programme.desc: When a beautiful teen with a bright future falls for a bad boy with a shady past, he tries to pull her into his life of crime. She has to decide how far she is willing to go for love and is forced to choose between her man and her moral compass.

programme.title: For My Man
programme.desc: A beautiful fashionista is swept off her feet when she meets a man who promises her the world. However, she discovers he has a dark secret. Consumed by love, she must decide how far she will go for him…and who she is willing to sacrifice.

programme.title: For My Man
programme.desc: After a painful divorce, a single mother didn’t expect to ever love again. But after a charming stranger wins her heart, she becomes desperate to do anything to keep her new man’s love – including betraying the innocent ones she cares for the most.

programme.title: For My Man
programme.desc: When a single mother meets a suave and loving provider, she looks the other way when she learns the source of his income. But the cops are intent on bringing her man down, and she becomes ensnared in a trap meant for him - paying the ultimate price.

programme.title: For My Man
programme.desc: When a woman with a checkered past meets a man with a similar history, sparks fly. But old habits die hard, and when her man falls back into a life of crime, she must decide to either follow her man into darkness�or to finally do the right thing.

programme.title: Fatal Attraction-Dupe
programme.desc: A young woman and her family were living the American Dream, but it shatters when the father vanishes, and the daughter goes silent. And when a body is discovered, investigators are led to a dark truth that devastates their once-happy lives.

programme.title: Fatal Attraction-Dupe
programme.desc: After embarking on a new romance, a recent divorcee is found strangled at home. Detectives suspect her new boyfriend, but when another woman is murdered under eerily similar circumstances, they uncover the horrific pattern of a deceptive killer.

programme.title: Atlanta Homicide
programme.desc: When the skull of 11-year-old Alan Watson is found in a field, Quinn and Velazquez take the case of a murder that took place almost a decade before.

programme.title: Atlanta Homicide
programme.desc: Mother Shaquilla Weatherspoon is found murdered with her belongings scattered around her. To the trained eye, it looks like the killer staged the crime scene. programme.desc: After a young man with a criminal record is shot and killed in broad daylight, investigators have to figure out if the shooting was random, or if his death may have been the work of a player from his troubled past intent on payback.

programme.desc: Technology is not only becoming an increasingly important part of people's lives but also of the American economy. "Bloomberg Technology" takes a look at how the latest happenings in the tech industry are affecting the U.S. markets. Analysis, commentary, interviews and interactive graphics are among the staples of the show that help viewers understand the impact the technology sector has on the economy. The show, which was originally titled "Bloomberg West," is anchored by Emily Chang in San Francisco.

programme.title: Bloomberg Real Yield
programme.desc: Not everyone has a lot of money to invest, which is why Bloomberg has financial indices that are based on fixed income, which is also the focus on this weekly show. The program covers news and trends in the fixed-income market and includes interviews with guests from major financial institutions. "Bloomberg Real Yield" incorporates data and analysis from terminal bond experts to help dive into topics that are moving the bond markets in order to help empower viewers to make knowledgeable financial decisions.

programme.title: The David Rubenstein Show: Peer to Peer Conversations
programme.desc: Financier and philanthropist David Rubenstein talks with his peers -- leaders of some of America's biggest companies -- in this interview show that aims to answer the question of what makes a great leader. Rubenstein uses his chats to explore successful leadership skills through the personal and professional choices made by the business world's most influential people. Rubenstein travels across the country to find out about the business leaders' stories and uncover information about their paths to success. The interview subjects include Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates, Berkshire Hathaway chairman Warren Buffett, and Pepsi CEO Indra Nooyi.

programme.title: LEADERS with Francine Lacqua:Alexander Stubb, President of Finland
programme.desc: What drives the world’s most influential minds? In Leaders with Francine Lacqua, Bloomberg’s award-winning anchor sits down with the decision-makers shaping our future to explore the personal stories behind their public personas. Discover what inspires them, how they navigate power and the lessons they offer on defining success. Insightful, cinematic and elegantly crafted, Leaders goes beyond the headlines to reveal the human side of leadership.

programme.title: Cigarrito Bañado
programme.desc: Lucio huye de una deuda y de un amigo peligroso. En el camino conoce a Ximena, una mujer que seduce a los hombres y luego les roba el dinero.

programme.title: Cometa
programme.desc: Un chico pierde todas las cosas que lo motivan a vivir. Se ha quedado sin trabajo, casa y familia, pero en su recorrido por las calles se encuentra a Cometa, un perro callejero.

programme.title: Mariachi gringo
programme.desc: Edward, un apasionado por la música mexicana, se siente atrapado en la rutina de la pequeña ciudad donde reside. Con el propósito de huir de todo, Edward se muda a México para convertirse en un mariachi.

programme.title: Soy de la frontera verde
programme.desc: Don Hugo un poderoso narcotraficante, muere en circunstacias muy extrañas, aunque todo indica que lo envenenaron. Su hija, doña Fernanda Morales, no dejará cabos sueltos y vengará la muerte de su padre encontrando al culpable.

programme.title: Un ángel en peligro
programme.desc: La felicidad de una familia se quiebra cuando secuestran a su hija. Al mismo tiempo, se descubrirá la red de engaños entre su sobrino y su esposa, además de un nexo con un narcotraficante.

programme.title: El Laberinto de Alicia
programme.desc: Rafael y Sofía involucran a Francisco en la misteriosa muerte de Diana. Mateo sabe quién es el Gato, pero no dice nada.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Said le confiesa a Jade que ha tratado de sacarla de su corazón, sin lograrlo. Ella se conmueve, baila la danza del sable y viven una noche de pasión. Lucas se molesta por el collar de Marisa.

programme.title: Mi amor de las estrellas
programme.desc: Song-yi insiste en devolver a Min-Joon a su planeta. Cuando va a cenar con la familia de la actriz, la madre de Song-yi descubre que es una extraterrestre.

programme.title: Génesis
programme.desc: Adaptación de uno de los libros más reconocidos de la Biblia, que aborda la historia del origen de la humanidad, invitando a entender el presente, volviendo al principio de todo. La caída de Lucifer, la vida de Adán y Eva, Noé y el diluvio, y otros.

programme.title: Señora del Destino
programme.desc: María do Carmo es una madre de cinco hijos que triunfó en la vida con mucha lucha y tesón, pero que todavía tendrá una batalla que luchar: obtener el cariño de su hija quien fue secuestrada cuando era una recién nacida.

programme.title: Melissa
programme.desc: Fevzi cree haber encontrado a su hija e intenta acercarse a Melissa. Cüneyt y Mahinur reviven su juventud durante las vacaciones. Melissa y Görkem piensan que Defne oculta algo. Cüneyt debe vender la finca.

programme.title: El laberinto de Alicia
programme.desc: Alicia Vega, psicóloga del colegio Los Ángeles, especialista en prevención y tratamiento de abusos sexuales a menores, inicia una investigación cuando Valentina, una niña adorable y frágil, parece estar en la mira de un abusador.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Cristina se da cuenta de que se casó con otro solo por darle celos a su ex. Tres años después vuelve a Miami sin un peso y busca a Leonardo. Latiffa está embarazada, Mohamed está feliz.

programme.title: Mi amor de las estrellas
programme.desc: Min-joon profesa su amor por Song-yi y luego desaparece. Con el paso del tiempo, Song-yi y otros creen que lo han visto.

programme.desc: Melissa queda devastada al saber que Görkem debe marcharse y confronta a Fevzi. Necati intenta usar a Reyhan para manipular a Cüneyt. Orhan amenaza a Büsra si se atreve a revelar lo que sabe. Mahinur y Metin temen por la seguridad de Melissa.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: As the Celtics prep for Game 4 of the NBA Finals we revisit some of Chronicle’s many behind-the-scenes moments with the Green! Shayna Seymour has bowled with Marcus Smart, taken a Duck Tour with Robert Williams, and sat down with (then-rookie) Jayson Tatum - and his mom! We recall the time the whole Chronicle crew took on Garden duties for a day. And back in the glory days of the ‘80s we got an inside look at a Celtics practice. Get pumped up for the playoffs with our trip down Celtics - and Chronicle! - memory lane.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: We packed a lot into our 40th anniversary special but there’s so much more in the vault! Tonight, we dive back into the archives to revisit some truly “classic” moments – from the time the whole crew took shifts at a New England country inn, to an interview with the local couple who dressed alike every day of their married life, to our TWO interviews with Oprah Winfrey. Throw in our visit to a “Dirty-Dancing”-style resort in the Catskills and you have yourself a Classic. Tonight we chronicle more memorable people, places, and adventures we’ve brought you over the years.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Ted Reinstein makes a spring visit to Maine’s rocky coast and explores the historic town bounded by Casco Bay and the Androscoggin River.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: In honor of St. Patrick’s Day (and our 40th anniversary this year) we’re making a virtual “return trip” to the Emerald Isle. Longtime co-anchors Mary Richardson and Peter Mehegan traveled the “Narrow Roads and Back Lanes” several times over the course of two decades, when Ireland experienced significant changes. What hasn’t changed? The raw beauty of the landscape. In tonight’s Classic Chronicle, we’ll take in the scenic lakes of Killarney, Peter’s search for his roots, and Mary’s inspirational visit to Donegal and trip on the River Shannon.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: We’re all about the Bay State “Meds” - Medford, Medway, and Medfield! We visit an Italian restaurant in Medford that’s all about family, and has a different take on a menu (hint: there isn’t one).

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Americans are obsessed with coffee and Nicole Estaphan is brewing up some good stories. She explores the history of coffee in Boston (was it coffee, not tea, that fueled the Revolution?) and meets the coffee trailblazer who founded the late, great Coffee Connection. Nicole learns latte art from the expert baristas at Ogawa in Boston, and if you love beer and coffee, at Marlborough “brew” house Lost Shoe you don’t have to choose! Finally, Nicole talks to a doctor who champions (moderate) coffee consumption.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: A local man who claims to have the world’s largest collection of Crocs (shoes, not reptiles). A Montreal man turned Bruins fanatic whose home is bursting with black-and-gold memorabilia. And a photographer who collects… sunsets (we’ll explain). Tonight, we meet New Englanders who just can’t resist the temptation to collect… well, something. They include a local couple making a star turn on their own home renovation show; they invite Anthony Everett into the massive collection of antiques in their barn.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: It influences our moods, our buying habits, and can even determine how hungry we are. We are talking about color! Nicole Estaphan takes us on a journey through the rainbow as we learn how these wavelengths impact our feelings. She takes us to MIT, where a team is working with fabric that can change color whenever you want. From “Tyrion Purple” to “Oregon Blue” - Nicole stops at the Harvard Museum’s Forbes Pigment Collection to learn from some of the oldest varieties on the planet. Finally, have a love for all those holiday light displays? Nicole takes us to the Marshfield Fairgrounds to see how color ties everything together.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight Anthony Everett and Shayna Seymour host an hour packed with gems from Chronicle’s vault, in honor of our 40th anniversary.

programme.title: WCVB 50 Years Special
programme.desc: (2022) WCVB Channel 5 celebrates 50 years - from the very first moments on the air to meeting the people who make it all happen.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Next time someone gives you a hard time about your pandemic diet (or lack thereof), ignore them and Flip the Bird...as in the Beverly restaurant, Flip the Bird, known for its variety of chicken sandwiches. Perhaps you’re a lobster lover or you prefer a plate of pasta for dinner? We’re trying them all tonight, along with spicy hot ramen, savory pies, and to wash it all down, hot Irish coffee.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chicken and dumplings. Shrimp and grits. Mac and cheese. Darryl’s Corner Bar and Kitchen in Roxbury whips up Southern comfort food staples, and we cozy up to the bar at Hunter’s Kitchen and Bar in South Boston. Erika Tarantal carbo-loads with warm, savory English muffins at Vinal Bakery in Somerville, while in Cambridge we indulge in heaps of ramen and Thai home cooking. Plus, we meet a baker in Providence whose pop-up croissant shop draws lines out the door.

programme.title: FIXT
programme.desc: Andrew returned home from serving his country with a toothache so bad he covers his mouth out of habit. Mickey had forgotten how to smile after years of struggle with addiction and abuse. Will the FIXT team help them smile again?

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: As the temperatures drop we are headed out in search of meals that warm our bellies, including warm and comforting Vietnamese noodle soups, hot chocolate, pumpkin gnocchi, and red sauce specialties.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Who says you have to cross state lines to find beautiful Main Streets and Back Roads? After all, there’s plenty to enjoy in our own backyard. Tonight, Anthony Everett heads to Concord, Massachusetts, a town with a rich concentration of history, culture, and natural beauty.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: People who say the Nutmeg State is boring just aren’t looking hard enough. This quiet corner of New England is home to a medieval castle (and its eccentric ruler), and the prized collection of the late “Nut Lady.”

programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: You don’t need your own beach to throw your own clambake! Tonight Shayna Seymour gets advice from local chefs and restaurateurs on prepping summer specialties: Backyard burgers from Bred Gourmet, steamers at Woodman’s of Essex, and super-fancy s’mores from Cambridge’s Urban Hearth. Shayna also meets a retired police chief who’s dishing up pizza in his “golden years.”

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: We still have some summer left to hold onto, and it’s definitely been a “scorch-ah”! We have some ideas if you need to “cool down” a bit. Ted Reinstein visits some of Vermont’s best swimming holes, while Anthony Everett strolls along the pier and sands of Old Orchard Beach in Maine. We sip wine slushies, healthy smoothies, and shaved ice cream (yes, it’s a thing) at local businesses. We also check out a local lake with the longest name on record, and hit a waterfall in a spot many don’t know is right nearby.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Treat yourself to cool desserts! Enjoy ice cream, gelato, slushies and more.

programme.title: The Best of Chronicle: Main Streets and Back Roads
programme.desc: New Hampshire’s Lakes Region is a renowned summer playground, but tonight we’re exploring the beauty of the surrounding “Cotton Valley” in springtime. We keep our eyes peeled for the traditional “ice out” and take in a model yacht race on Lake Winnipesaukee. We also meet the couple who saved a historic local home, transforming it into an inn recently named by Travel and Leisure as the number one resort hotel in the U.S. And at Pavilion, a restaurant led by Top Chef alum Chris Viaud, we sample scrumptious seasonal fare.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: School vacation is upon us, the temps are summer-like, and the New England shoreline is well within reach. From Cape Cod to the coast of Maine, tonight we take a return ride to some of our favorite “MSBR” summer destinations.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: New England has lost 10,000 dairy farms in the last 50 years, but today about 1,000 local farms are still producing milk, butter, and cheese – and innovating along the way. Tonight, Ted Reinstein visits four family-owned farms, three here in Massachusetts and one in New Hampshire, each with its own approach to surviving in a challenging industry, preserving family legacies, adapting new technologies to mitigate climate impact – and yes, producing delicious dairy products (including ice cream!).

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Dams can be found on just about every river in the Bay State. Some are enormous and serve the needs of millions while others crumble away. Some are quite lovely and others... less so. In fact, most dams in Massachusetts serve no purpose anymore. All of this has Ted Reinstein asking, what is the dam story? He gets to the bottom of it tonight.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Erika Tarantal hand-feeds giraffes and Anthony Everett joins the National Guard for a day as we try a few different careers on for size. Anthony gives us a look at the rigorous training that citizen-soldiers in the Mass. National Guard (“The Nation’s First”) undergo at Joint Base Cape Cod. Meanwhile, Erika tests whether she has what it takes to become a Gloucester firefighter, a guest services rep at the Encore Resort’s five-star hotel, and a zookeeper at the Franklin Park Zoo.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight, wallet-friendly things to do, see – and of course, eat! At the family-owned Beef Barn in Smithfield, R.I., a juicy roast beef sandwich comes in under 5 bucks. On Boston Common, you can commission a portrait of yourself for $1. I

programme.desc: Tonight we meet the people who are living with it; treating and researching it; and developing new technology to improve outcomes. We meet patients who describe life with diabetes, and discover new technology helping to monitor.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: The Asian food scene is booming in Greater Boston, with many local restaurants focusing on region-specific dishes. Anthony Everett samples Thai “tapas” at Ma Hani Yom in Brookline. We visit old friends at Burmese restaurant Yoma in Allston. The popular Instagrammer known as “Boston Food Bae” takes us to her favorite udon restaurant, Futago in Boston. And we indulge in Korean dish Bibimbap at food truck Bibim Box. Those stops and more on tonight’s food journey.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: From cheesy Salvadoran papusas to spongy Ethiopian injera, tonight Erika Tarantal digs into breads that are savory, sweet… and culturally unique.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Greater Boston you can still enjoy “dinner and a show.” At a Plymouth drive-in, movie-goers can catch a flick outdoors while enjoying elevated fare (Wagyu sriracha hot dogs, anyone?). On a Cambridge food tour, the “show” is the history and culture of the neighborhood. In Boston’s Fenway neighborhood, we find dinner and a country music show. Finally, expert hibachi chefs put on a fiery show at a local Japanese steakhouse.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: WCVB Local News
programme.desc: Get the latest news and weather from WCVB.

programme.title: Fighting for Freedom: The United States v. Hamid Hayat
programme.desc: 14 years after he was arrested on federal terrorism charges, a Northern California man is freed after his case is overturned.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Here’s a suggestion for beating the dog days of summer – stay inside watching a show about actual dogs! We visit outdoor dining destinations in Boston and Salem where you can bring your four-legged friend (including one that serves dogs their meals on a take-home frisbee).

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Service dogs, K-9 officers – even a four-legged doggie influencer with his own glam squad and “momager.” Tonight we meet some of these working dogs – as well as other animals with day jobs. At NEADS World Class Service Dogs we meet puppies prepping for a career of service. The “employees” of Goats to Go help businesses tame out-of-control vegetation, while the mini-horses of Lifting Spirits connect with people in need of comfort and support. We also visit a home for retired monkeys who can no longer work as service animals.

programme.title: FIXT
programme.desc: Andrew returned home from serving his country with a toothache so bad he covers his mouth out of habit. Mickey had forgotten how to smile after years of struggle with addiction and abuse. Will the FIXT team help them smile again?

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight we go backstage at local theaters, each with its own rich history – each celebrating a major milestone. Also tonight, a sit-down with jazz legend Herbie Hancock ahead of his recent performance at the Boch Center Wang Theater.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Erika Tarantal loves children, so it is no surprise some of her favorite stories this year were stories about children: keeping kids engaged with learning, giving them access to healthy activities, and connecting children to the environment. Tonight, a rundown of Erika’s 2021 highlights from her “Five for Good” series.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: It’s that time of year, when we look back at the stories we’ve shared with you throughout the year – and the generous people who have shared their stories with us. Erika Tarantal's favorites.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Nicole Estaphan brings us eye-catching, eye-popping, “eye candy!” She meets artist Howie Green, famed for his colorful “pop-up” pop art, and discovers shoes that will blow your mind (and benefit good causes). In Winsted, Conn. Nicole checks out a 5-story-high mural that took 22 years to complete. And for those who believe we “taste with our eyes,” Nicole visits Jonquils in Boston, where a Ukrainian-born pastry chef used 3-D technology to dream up modern desserts, and Confectionery Designs in Rehoboth, where the sweets are to-eye-for.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: You can’t eat these Tootsie Rolls or Necco wafers – they’re works of art by a bead artist whose work is featured at a local gallery. Tonight Erika Tarantal brings us beautiful things and the people who create them.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight we test whether New Englanders can agree on what qualifies as acceptable etiquette, from “manspreading” on the T to lane-cutting on Storrow Drive. Tune in to find out which very opinionated New Englander we invite to weigh in as our celebrity judge.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: A feast of Fall flavors! Pumpkin Coffee, Pumpkin Pizza, Pumpkin Donuts and more!

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight's episode is a feast of fall flavors.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: It's Spooky Season! Pumpkin carving, ghost adventures, Fall drinks and more!

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: There’s nothing quite like fall in New England and tonight we explore some of the best ways to enjoy the season.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: In Dorchester, a community newspaper is going strong thanks to a proud family legacy. In the North End, a local entrepreneur has turned her family’s longtime neighborhood pharmacy into an Italian restaurant. And one Needham restaurateur is so proud of his mother’s cake recipe that it graces the menu of his restaurant. Tonight, stories of families who choose to work together, and are committed to keeping business “all in the family.”

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: From “open barnyard” at a South Shore farm to art classes on Beacon Hill and the Dr. Seuss Museum in Springfield, tonight we explore local activities and sites that are family-friendly (but fun for everyone).

programme.title: Crimes, Cons & Capers
